Text:
Four breweries make their HBCF debut this year, joining the Cask Bar lineup for the first time. Bishop Nick, Ivo, ShinDigger and Bluntrock between them cover a wide range, from traditional cask styles to modern interpretations, and each brings something we have not had at the festival before. Swipe through to see who is new, and look out for their pump clips on the Cask Bar across all three days. Let us know in the comments if you find a new favourite.

Advance tickets are on sale now:
https://hitchinbeerciderfestival.org.uk/
or visit the link in our bio.

4th to 6th June 2026
King George V Playing Fields, Old Hale Way, Hitchin, SG5 1XL

#HitchinBeerFestival #HBCF2026 #RealAle #CAMRA #CaskAle #Hitchin #NorthHerts #Hertfordshire #SupportLocal #CommunityEvent

In partnership with CAMRA North Hertfordshire and Hitchin Rugby Club.

Text:
For the craft-curious, the Craft Bar this year brings together six brewers worth seeking out. Queer Brewing, Pretty Decent, The Kernel, Abbeydale, Baron, and Crossover Blendery will all be pouring across the weekend, between them covering hazy IPAs, stouts, mixed-fermentation farmhouse beers and plenty in between. The Craft Bar runs alongside the cask range, so you can move between the two as the mood takes you. Swipe to see the lineup, and let us know if you find a beer to remember.

Advance tickets are on sale now:
https://hitchinbeerciderfestival.org.uk/
or visit the link in our bio.

4th to 6th June 2026
King George V Playing Fields, Old Hale Way, Hitchin, SG5 1XL
